strategy: cf1 takes the required energy-constrained inspection order T0 -> T1 -> recharge at S1 -> T2 -> final. The S1 recharge is inserted after T1 because the pre-charge distance remains just above the 35% battery floor, and a 4 s hold restores the battery to the 100% cap before the longer return leg. Transit waypoints use the upper/top and bottom corridors around the inflated obstacle boxes, with high-altitude detours to give the smoother enough room for gentle turns. cf2 starts at S1 after cf1's intended later arrival, visits T3 via the open right/bottom corridor, and finishes near the lower goal. The two routes are spatially separated for most of the mission; cf1 uses the top corridor first while cf2 moves down the right/bottom side, and cf1's only visit to S1 is scheduled by the optimizer after its first two targets, reducing station conflict.
